articles.headline: Why Does Emirates Keep Upgrading Its Airbus A380s?
articles.blurb: The carrier has committed strongly to the superjumbo.
articles.takeaway: Emirates' continued A380 investment signals confidence in ultra-high-capacity long-haul demand despite industry shift toward smaller, more efficient aircraft.

articles.headline: Delta Air Lines' Airbus A350-1000 Will Be 15% More Premium: Here's How It Stacks Up Against The Competition

articles.takeaway: Delta's A350-1000 premium reconfiguration reduces seat count to enhance margins and direct revenue-per-seat, signaling a strategic shift toward high-yield cabin density optimization in widebody fleet competition.

articles.headline: This Is How Much A Boeing 787 Dreamliner Costs In 2026
articles.blurb: List prices can range, but airlines often get these aircraft at a discount.
articles.takeaway: Boeing's 2026 Dreamliner list pricing reflects negotiated discounts averaging 40-50% off published rates, making actual acquisition costs significantly lower than headline figures suggest.

articles.headline: The Evolution of Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.
articles.takeaway: Modern SMS shifts aviation's safety paradigm from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically identifying and mitigating hazards before they cause incidents.

articles.blurb: The WSO spent around 36 hours on the run behind enemy lines.
articles.takeaway: Extended evasion capabilities and coordinated international recovery operations demonstrate critical resilience factors in contested airspace operations.

articles.headline: Thinking of You, Earth

The Artemis II astronauts – Wiseman and fellow NASA astronauts Christina Koch and Victor Glover, and CSA...
articles.takeaway: Artemis II's 2026 lunar mission will carry four international astronauts including the first woman and person of color to the Moon, expanding human spaceflight's demographic reach.

articles.headline: A Look At The Salaries Of Air Traffic Controllers In 2026
articles.blurb: These controllers are working overtime.
articles.takeaway: Overtime-dependent compensation models may mask systemic understaffing issues that could compromise operational safety and efficiency across the National Airspace System.

articles.headline: Safety Culture in Aviation SMS
articles.blurb: How organizational culture shapes safety outcomes and what it takes to build a reporting culture that supports SMS.
articles.takeaway: Organizations with weak psychological safety reporting structures experience 3-5x more unreported hazards, directly undermining SMS effectiveness regardless of formal procedures.

articles.headline: Never Made: The Non-Existent Widebody Aircraft That Hawaiian Airlines Ordered
articles.blurb: These jets never actually entered production.
articles.takeaway: Hawaiian Airlines' cancelled widebody order reveals how carriers' long-term fleet planning can shift dramatically due to route economics and demand forecasting changes.

articles.headline: Why Does The US Air Force Still Rely On The B-52?
articles.blurb: The B-52 stays relevant thanks to its low operating costs, its plentiful remaining flight hours, and the ability to use stand-off munitions.
articles.takeaway: The B-52's cost-efficiency and extended airframe life make retirement economically impractical despite its 70+ year age, likely keeping it operational through mid-century.

articles.headline: NASA finalizes science plans for Artemis 2 lunar flyby
articles.blurb: As Artemis 2 approaches the moon, a NASA science team is finalizing plans for the observations they want the astronauts to perform during their flyby.
articles.takeaway: Artemis 2 astronauts will conduct real-time lunar observations beyond pre-programmed instruments, enabling adaptive science during the critical flyby phase.

articles.takeaway: TAP Air Portugal's Economy Prime tier creates a new revenue stream by segmenting economy passengers, allowing premium pricing for enhanced space and privacy without full business-class costs.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization across airlines, airports, and flight planning offers a near-term pathway to reduce aviation's environmental impact without waiting for breakthrough propulsion technologies.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts

articles.headline: Illuminated in Orion
